info.title: Stryker
info.description: The Stryker.NET CLI tool. Install this tool to run stryker from the commandline.

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help information.

options.name: --config-file
options.description: Choose the file containing your stryker configuration relative to current working directory. Supports json and yaml formats. | default: stryker-config.json

options.name: --skip-version-check
options.description: Skips check for newer version. | default: false

options.name: --break-at
options.description: Anything below this mutation score will return a non-zero exit code. Must be less than or equal to threshold low. | default: '0' | allowed: 0 - 100

options.name: --threshold-high
options.description: Minimum good mutation score. Must be higher than or equal to threshold low. | default: '80' | allowed: 0 - 100

options.name: --threshold-low
options.description: Minimum acceptable mutation score. Must be less than or equal to threshold high and more than or equal to threshold break. | default: '60' | allowed: 0 - 100

options.name: --log-to-file
options.description: Makes the logger write to a file. Logging to file always uses loglevel trace. | default: 'False'

options.name: --verbosity
options.description: The verbosity (loglevel) for output to the console. | default: 'info' | allowed: error, warning, info, debug, trace

options.name: --concurrency
options.description:
By default Stryker tries to make the most of your CPU, by spawning as many parallel processes as you have CPU cores.
This setting allows you to override this default behavior.
Reasons you might want to lower this setting:
- Your test runner starts a browser (another CPU-intensive process)
- You're running on a shared server
- You are running stryker in the background while doing other work | default: '2'

options.name: --disable-bail
options.description: Disable abort unit testrun as soon as the first unit test fails. | default: 'False'

options.name: --solution
options.description: Full path to your solution file. Required on dotnet framework.

options.name: --configuration
options.description: Configuration to use when building the project(s) (e.g., 'Debug' or 'Release'). If not specified, the default configuration of the project(s) will be used.

options.name: --project
options.description: Used to find the project to test in the project references of the test project. Example: "ExampleProject.csproj" | default: ''

options.name: --test-project
options.description: Specify the test projects. | default: []

options.name: --msbuild-path
options.description: The path to the msbuild executable to use to build your .NET Framework application. Not used for .net (core).

options.name: --target-framework
options.description: The framework to build the project against.

options.name: --mutate
options.description:
Allows to specify file that should in- or excluded for the mutations.
Use glob syntax for wildcards: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Glob_(programming)
Use '!' at the start of a pattern to exclude all matched files.
Use '{<start>..<end>}' at the end of a pattern to specify spans of text in files to in- or exclude.
Example: ['**/*Service.cs','!**/MySpecialService.cs', '**/MyOtherService.cs{1..10}{32..45}'] | default: ['**/*']

options.name: --mutation-level
options.description: Specify which mutation levels to place. Every higher level includes the mutations from the lower levels. | default: 'Standard' | allowed: Basic, Standard, Advanced, Complete

options.name: --since
options.description: Enables diff compare. Only test changed files. | default: 'False'

options.name: --with-baseline
options.description: EXPERIMENTAL: Use results stored in stryker dashboard to only test new mutants. | default: 'False'

options.name: --open-report
options.description: When this option is passed, generated reports should open in the browser automatically once Stryker starts testing mutants, and will update the report till Stryker is done. Both html and dashboard reports can be opened automatically. | default: 'Html' | allowed: Html, Dashboard

options.name: --reporter
options.description: Reporters inform about various stages in the mutation testrun. | default: ['Progress', 'Html'] | allowed: All, Progress, Dots, ClearText, ClearTextTree, Json, Html, Dashboard, RealTimeDashboard, Markdown, Baseline

options.name: --version
options.description: Project version used in dashboard reporter and baseline feature. | default: ''

options.name: --dashboard-api-key
options.description: Api key for dashboard reporter.

options.name: --azure-fileshare-sas
options.description: A Shared Access Signature for Azure File Storage is required when Azure File Storage is used for dashboard compare. For more information: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/common/storage-sas-overview | default: ''

options.name: --s3-bucket-name
options.description: The name of the S3 bucket to store baseline reports in. Required when the S3 baseline provider is selected. | default: ''

options.name: --s3-endpoint
options.description: Custom endpoint URL for S3-compatible storage (e.g. MinIO, Backblaze B2). Optional; when not set the default AWS S3 endpoint is used. | default: ''

options.name: --s3-region
options.description: The region for the S3 bucket (e.g. us-east-1). Optional; when not set the SDK resolves the region from environment variables or profile. | default: ''

options.name: --output
options.description: Changes the output path for Stryker logs and reports. This can be an absolute or relative path.

options.name: --break-on-initial-test-failure
options.description: Instruct Stryker to break execution when at least one test failed on initial run. | default: 'False'

options.name: --diag
options.description:
Stryker enters diagnostic mode. Useful when encountering issues.
Setting this flag makes Stryker increase the debug level and log more information to help troubleshooting. | default: 'False'

options.name: --test-runner
options.description: Specify the test runner to use. | default: 'vstest' | allowed: vstest, mtp

options.name: --config-file
options.description: Choose the file containing your stryker configuration relative to current working directory. Supports json and yaml formats. | default: stryker-config.json

options.name: --skip-version-check
options.description: Skips check for newer version. | default: false

options.name: --threshold-high

options.name: --threshold-low
options.description: Minimum acceptable mutation score. Must be less than or equal to threshold high and more than or equal to threshold break. | default: '60' | allowed: 0 - 100

options.description: Makes the logger write to a file. Logging to file always uses loglevel trace. | default: 'False'
